author: John Chrysostom
book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
Homily II. On the Power of Man to Resist the Devil.

title: Homily II. On the Power of Man to Resist the Devil.

source.title: NPNF1-09. St. Chrysostom: On the Priesthood; Ascetic Treatises; Select Homilies and Letters; Homilies on the Statutes

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Against those who object because the devil has not been put out of the world: and to prove that his wickedness does no harm to us—if we take heed: and concerning repentance.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: When Isaac, in old time, was desirous to eat a meal at the hands of his son, he sent his son forth from the house to the chace.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But when this Isaac was desirous to accept a meal at my hands he did not send me forth from the house, but himself ran to our table.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What could be more tenderly affectionate than he?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What more humble? who thought fit to shew his warm love thus, and deigned to descend so far.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: On this account surely, we also having spent the tones of our voice, and the strength of our feet over the morning discourse, when we saw his fatherly face, forgot our weakness, lay aside our fatigue, were uplifted with pleasure; we saw his illustrious hoary head, and our soul was filled with light.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: On this account too, we set out our table with readiness, in order that he should eat and bless us.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: There is no fraud and guile, here, as there was then, there.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: One indeed was commanded to bring the meal—but another brought it.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But I was commanded to bring it, and brought it too.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Bless me then, O my father, with spiritual blessing, which we all also pray ever to receive, and which is profitable not only to thee, but also to me, and to all these.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Entreat the common master of us all, to prolong thy life to the old age of Isaac.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For this is both for me, and for these, more valuable, and more needful than the dew of heaven, and the fatness of the earth.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But it is time to proceed to set out our table; what then is this?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The remains of what was lately said with a view to our love of you.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For still—still—we renew our discourse concerning the Devil, which we started two days ago, which we also addressed to the initiated, this morning when we discoursed to them about renunciation, and covenant.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And we do this, not because our discourse about the Devil is sweet to us, but because the doctrine about him is full of security for you.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For he is an enemy and a foe, and it is a great security to know clearly, the tactics of your enemies.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: We have said lately, that he does not overcome by force, nor by tyranny, nor through compulsion, nor through violence.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Since were this so, he would have destroyed all men.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And in testimony of this we brought forward the swine, against which the Demons were unable to venture anything, before the permission of the Master.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The herds and flocks of Job.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For not even did the Devil venture to destroy these, until he received power from above.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: We learned therefore this one thing first, that he does not overcome us by force, or by compulsion; next after that, we added that even when he overcomes by deceitfulness, not thus does he get the better of all men, Then again we brought that athlete Job, himself into the midst, against whom he set countless schemes going, and not even thus got the better of him, but withdrew defeated.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: One question still remains.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What then is this matter?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: That if he does not overcome says one, by force, yet by deceitfulness.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And on this account it were better that he should be destroyed.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For if Job got the better of him, yet Adam was deceived and overthrown.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Now if once for all he had been removed from the world, Adam would never have been overthrown.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But now he remains, and is defeated indeed by one, but gets the better of many.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Ten overcame him, but he himself overcomes and wrestles down ten thousand and if God took him away from the world, these ten thousand would not have perished.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What then shall we say to this?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: That first of all they who overcame are more valuable far than they who are defeated, even if the latter be more, and the former less.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: “For better is one,” saith he “that doeth the will of God than ten thousand transgressors.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And next, that if the antagonist were taken away he who overcomes is thereby injured.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For if thou lettest the adversary remain, the more slothful are injured, not on account of the more diligent, but by their own slothfulness; whereas if thou takest away the antagonist, the more diligent are betrayed on account of the slothful, and neither exhibit their own power, nor win crowns.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Perhaps ye have not yet understood what has been said.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Therefore it is necessary that I should say it again more clearly.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Let there be one antagonist.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But let there be also two athletes about to wrestle against him, and of these two athletes let one be consumed with gluttony, unprepared, void of strength, nerveless; but the other diligent, of good habit, passing his time in the wrestling school, in many gymnastic exercises, and exhibiting all the practice which bears upon the contest.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: If then thou takest away the antagonist, which of these two hast thou injured?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The slothful, pray, and unprepared, or the earnest one who has toiled so much?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: It is quite clear that it is the earnest one: For the one indeed is wronged by the slothful, after the antagonist has been taken away.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But the slothful, while he remains, is no longer injured on account of the earnest.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For he has fallen, owing to his own slothfulness.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: I will state another solution of this question, in order that thou mayest learn, that the Devil does not injure, but their own slothfulness everywhere overthrows those who do not take heed.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Let the Devil be allowed to be exceeding wicked, not by nature, but by choice and conviction.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For that the Devil is not by nature wicked, learn from his very names.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For the Devil, the slanderer that is, is called so from slandering; for he slandered man to God saying “Doth Job reverence thee for nought? but put out thine hand, and touch what he hath, see if he will not blaspheme thee to thy face.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: He slandered God again to man saying “Fire fell from heaven and burnt up the sheep.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For he was anxious to persuade him, that this warfare was stirred up from above, out of the heavens, and he set the servant at variance with the master, and the master with his servant; rather he did not set them at variance, but attempted to indeed, but was not able, in order that whenever thou mayest set another servant at variance with his master, Adam with God, and believing the Devil’s slander, thou mayest learn that he gained strength, not owing to his own power but from that man’s slothfulness and carelessness.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: He is called the Devil therefore on that account.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But to slander, and to refrain from slander is not natural, but an action which takes place and which ceases to take place, occurring and ceasing to occur.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Now such things do not reach the rank of the nature or of the essence of a thing.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: I know that this consideration about essence and accident is hard to be grasped by many.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But there are they who are able to lend a finer ear, wherefore also we have spoken these things.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Do you wish that I should come to another name?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: You shall see that that also is not a name which belongs to his essence or nature.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: He is called wicked.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But his wickedness is not from his nature, but from his choice.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For even this at one time is present, at another time is absent.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Do not thou then say this to me that it always remains with him.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For it was not indeed with him at the beginning, but afterwards came upon him; wherefore he is called apostate.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Although many men are wicked, he alone is called wicked by pre-eminence.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Why then is he thus called?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Because though in no way wronged by us, having no grudge whether small or great, when he saw mankind had in honour, he straightway envied him his good.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What therefore could be worse than this wickedness, except when hatred and war exist, without having any reasonable cause.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Let the Devil then be let alone, and let us bring forward the creation, in order that thou mayest learn that the Devil is not the cause of ills to us, if we would only take heed: in order that thou mayest learn that the weak in choice, and the unprepared, and slothful, even were there no Devil, falls, and casts himself into many a depth of evil.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The Devil is evil.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: I know it myself and it is acknowledged by all, yet give heed strictly to the things which are now about to be said.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For they are not ordinary matters, but those about which many words, many times, and in many places arise, about which there is many a fight and battle not only on the part of the faithful against unbelievers but also on the part of the faithful against the faithful.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For this is that which is full of pain.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The Devil then is acknowledged, as I said, to be evil by all.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What shall we say about this beautiful and wondrous creation?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Pray is the creation too, wicked? and who is so corrupt, who so dull, and demented as to accuse the creation? what then shall we say about this?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For it is not wicked, but is both beautiful and a token of the wisdom and power and lovingkindness of God.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Hear at least how the prophet marvels at it, saying, “How are thy works magnified O Lord! in wisdom Thou hast made them all.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: He did go through them one by one, but withdrew before the incomprehensible wisdom of God.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And that he has made it thus beautiful and vast hear a certain one saying, “From the vastness and beauty of the creatures, the originator of them is proportionably seen.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Hear too Paul saying, “For the invisible things of Him, since the creation of the world, are clearly seen, being perceived through the things that are made.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For each of these by which he spake declared that the creation leads us to the knowledge of God, because it causes us to know the Master fully.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: If we see this beautiful and wondrous creation itself becoming a cause of impiety to many, shall we blame it?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: In no wise, but them who were unable to use the medicine rightly.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Whence then is this which leads us to the knowledge of God, a cause of impiety?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: “The wise” saith he “were darkened in their understandings, and worshipped and served the creature more than the creator.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The Devil is nowhere here, a Demon is nowhere here, but the creation alone is set before us, as the teacher of the knowledge of God.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: How then has it become the cause of impiety?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Not owing to its own nature, but owing to the carelessness of those who do not take heed.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Shall we take away even the creation? tell me.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And why do I speak about the creation?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Let us come to our own members.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For even these we shall find to be a cause of destruction if we do not take heed, not because of their own nature, but because of our sloth.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And look; an eye was given, in order that thou mayest behold the creation and glorify the Master.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But if thou dost not use the eye well, it becomes to thee the minister of adultery.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: A tongue has been given, in order that thou mayest speak well, in order that thou mayest praise the Creator.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But if thou givest not excellent heed, it becomes a cause of blasphemy to thee.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And hands were given thee that thou mayest stretch them forth unto prayer.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But if thou are not wary, thou stretchest them out unto covetousness.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Feet were given in order that thou mayest run unto good works, but if thou art careless thou wilt cause wicked works by means of them: Dost thou see that all things hurt the weak man?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Dost thou see that even the medicines of salvation inflict death upon the weak, not because of their own nature but because of his weakness?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: God made the heaven in order that thou mayest wonder at the work, and worship the master.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But others leaving the creator alone, have worshipped the heaven; and this from their own carelessness and senselessness.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But why do I speak of the creation? assuredly what could be more conducive to salvation than the Cross?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But this Cross has become an offence to the weak.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: “For the word of the Cross is to them that are perishing, foolishness: but to those which are being saved, it is the power of God.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And again, “we preach Christ crucified, unto Jews a stumbling-block and unto Gentiles foolishness.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What could be more fit for teaching than Paul, and the apostles?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But the Apostles became a savour of death to many.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: He says at least “to one a savour from death unto death: to the other a savour from life unto life.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Dost thou see that the weak is hurt even by Paul, but the strong is injured not even by the Devil?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Dost thou wish that we should exercise the argument in the case of Jesus Christ?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What is equal to that salvation? what more profitable than that presence?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But this very saving presence, so profitable, became an additional means of chastening to many.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: “For for judgment” saith he “came I into this world, that they which see not may see, and that they which see may become blind.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What dost thou say?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The light became a cause of blindness?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The light did not become a cause of blindness, but the weakness of the eyes of the soul was not able to entertain the light.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Thou hast seen that a weak man is hurt on all sides, but the strong is benefited on all sides.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For in every case, the purpose is the cause, in every case the disposition is master.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Since the Devil, if thou wouldest understand it, is even profitable to us, if we use him aright, and benefits us greatly, and we gain no ordinary advantages; and this, we shewed in a small degree from the case of Job.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And it is possible also to learn this from Paul: for writing about the fornicator he thus speaks “Deliver such an one unto Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Behold even the Devil has become a cause of salvation, but not because of his own disposition, but because of the skill of the Apostle.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For as the physicians taking serpents and cutting off their destructive members, prepare medicines for antidotes; so also did Paul.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: He took whatever was profitable of the chastening that proceeds from the Devil, and left the rest alone; in order that thou mayest learn that the Devil is not the cause of salvation, but that he hasted to destroy and devour mankind.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But that the Apostle through his own wisdom cut his throat: hear in the second epistle to the Corinthians, what he saith about this very fornicator, “confirm your love towards him,” “lest by any means such an one should be swallowed up by over much sorrow.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And, “we be taken advantage of by Satan.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: We have snatched beforehand the man from the gullet of the wild beast, he saith.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For the Apostle often used the Devil as an executioner.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For the executioners punish those who have done wrong, not as they choose, but as the judges allow.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For this is the rule for the executioner, to take vengeance, giving heed to the command of the judge.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Dost thou see to what a dignity the Apostle mounted?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: He who was invested with a body, used the bodiless as an executioner; and that which their common master saith to the Devil, concerning Job: charging him thus, “Touch his flesh, but thou shalt not touch his life;” giving him a limit, and measure of vengeance, in order that the wild beast might not be impetuous and leap upon him too shamelessly; this too the Apostle does.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For delivering the fornicator over to him he says “For the destruction of the flesh,” that is “thou shalt not touch his life.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Dost thou see the authority of the servant?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Fear not therefore the Devil, even if he be bodiless: for he has come in contact with him.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And nothing is weaker than he who has come into such contact even though he be not invested with a body, as then nothing is stronger than he who has boldness even though he bear about a mortal body.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: All these things have been now said by me, not in order that I may discharge the Devil from blame, but that I may free you from slothfulness.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For he wishes extremely to attribute the cause of our sins to himself, in order that we being nourished by these hopes, and entering on all kinds of evil, may increase the chastening in our own case, and may meet with no pardon from having transferred the cause to him.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Just as Eve met with none.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But let us not do this.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But let us know ourselves.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Let us know our wounds.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For thus shall we be able to apply the medicines.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For he who does not know his disease, will give no care to his weakness.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: We have sinned much: I know this well.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For we are all liable for penalties.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But we are not deprived of pardon; nor shall we fall away from repentance for we still stand in the arena, and are in the struggles of repentance.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Art thou old, and hast thou come to the last outlet of life?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Do not consider even thus that thou hast fallen from repentance, nor despair of thine own salvation, but consider the robber who was freed on the cross.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For what was briefer than that hour in which he was crowned?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Yet notwithstanding even this was enough for him, for salvation.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Art thou young?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Do not be confident in thy youth, nor think that thou hast a very fixed term of life, “For the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: On this account he has made our end invisible, in order that we might make our diligence and our forethought plain.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Dost thou not see men taken away prematurely day after day?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: On this account a certain one admonishes “make no tarrying to turn to the Lord and put not off from day to day,” lest at any time, as thou delayest, thou art destroyed.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Let the old man keep this admonition, let the young man take this advice.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Yea, art thou in security, and art thou rich, and dost thou abound in wealth, and does no affliction happen to thee?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Still hear what Paul says “when they say peace and safety, then sudden destruction cometh upon them.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Affairs are full of much change.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: We are not masters of our end.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Let us be masters of virtue.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Our Master Christ is loving.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Do you wish that I shall speak of the ways of repentance?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: They are many, and various, and different, and all lead to heaven.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The first way of repentance is condemnation of sins.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: “Declare thou first thy sins that thou mayest be justified.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Wherefore also the prophet said “I said, I will speak out, my transgression to the Lord, and thou remittedst the iniquity of my heart.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Condemn thyself therefore for thy sins.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: This is enough for the Master by way of self-defence.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For he who condemns his sins, is slower to fall into them again.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Awake thy conscience, that inward accuser, in order that thou mayest have no accuser at the judgment seat of the Lord.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: This is one way of repentance, the best; and there is another not less than this, not to bear a grudge against thine enemies to overcome anger, to forgive the sins of our fellow-servants.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For so will those which have been done against the master be forgiven us.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: See the second expiation of sins: “For if ye forgive” saith he, “your debtors, your Heavenly Father will also forgive you.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to learn a third way of repentance?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Fervent and diligent prayer, and to do this from the bottom of the heart.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Hast thou not seen that widow, how she persuaded the shameless judge?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: But thou hast a gentle Master, both tender, and kind.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: She asked, against her adversaries, but thou dost not ask against thine adversaries, but on behalf of thine own salvation.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And if thou wouldest learn a fourth way, I will say almsgiving.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For this has a great power and unspeakable.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For Daniel saith to Nebuchadnezzar when he had come to all kinds of evil, and had entered upon all impiety, “O King let my counsel be acceptable unto thee, redeem thy sins by almsgiving and thine iniquities by compassion on the poor.”

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: What could be compared with this lovingkindness?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: After countless sins, after so many transgressions, he is promised that he will be reconciled with him he has come into conflict with if he will show kindness to his own fellow-servants.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And modesty, and humility, not less than all words spoken, exhaust the nature of sins.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And the publican is proof, being unable to declare his good deeds, in sight of all, bringing forward his humility, and laying aside the heavy burden of his sins.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: See we have shewn five ways of repentance: first the condemnation of sins, next the forgiveness of our neighbours’ sins, thirdly that which comes of prayer, fourth that which comes of almsgiving, fifth that which comes of humility.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Do not thou then be lazy; but walk in all these day by day.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: For the ways are easy, nor canst thou plead poverty.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And even if thou livest poorer than all, thou art able to leave thine anger, and be humble, and to pray fervently, and to condemn sins, and thy poverty is in no way a hindrance.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: And why do I speak thus, when not even in that way of repentance in which it is possible to spend money (I speak of almsgiving), not even there is poverty any hindrance to us from obeying the command?

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: The widow who spent the two mites is a proof.

verses.book: Three Homilies Concerning the Power of Demons.
verses.text: Having learned then the healing of our wounds, let us constantly apply these medicines, in order that we may return to health and enjoy the sacred table with assurance; and with much glory, reach Christ the king of glory, and attain to everlasting good by the grace, and compassion, and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom be glory, power, honour, to the Father, together with the all holy, and good and quickening Spirit, now and always and for ever and ever.
